name: Four-Day Vegetable Soup by James Beard, Chef
recipeIngredient: 2 cups cabbage, cooked and finely chopped
4 Swiss chard leaves and stems, chopped
4 rosemary leaves, fresh (or 1 teaspoon dried rosemary, crushed)
1 medium tomato, peeled; seeded and finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine all vegetables and rosemary in a deep pan.
2. Cover with water and add salt and pepper to taste—about 1 tablespoon salt and 1 teaspoon pepper.
3. Bring mixture to a boil gradually over low heat.
4. Cover the pot and simmer gently for 1-1/2 to 2 hours, or until all flavors are well blended.
5. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
6. Serve with crackers, cheese, or other accompaniments.
Variations:
First day: Enough soup for four people. Serve as described above.
Second day: Add another couple of peeled and chopped tomatoes, a few leaves of finely chopped spinach, an additional zucchini, and any leftover cooked chickpeas. Add grated lemon rind and a touch of onion about 10 minutes before serving.
Third day: The soup is good cold with a dollop of crème fraîche.
Fourth day: Add 2 or 3 peeled and diced beets, another cup of finely chopped cooked cabbage, 3 or 4 more mushrooms, and any leftover vegetable broth.
